Catalog Description

Advanced Hardware Design Functional Verification. (3-0). Credit 3. Advanced topics

in hardware functional verification; case studies on verification in integrated circuit

design; exposure to advanced industry best practices; focus on functional

verification in an IP-based hardware design methodology. Prerequisites: CSCE 616,

CSCE 312, CSCE 350, ECEN 350, ECEN 468, or equivalent; familiarity with

C/C++/Verilog/VHDL programming.

Course Prerequisites

Prerequisite/Corequisite(s):  CSCE 616, CSCE 312, CSCE 350/ECEN 350,

ECEN 350/CSCE 350, ECEN 468, or equivalent; familiarity with C/C++/Verilog/VHDL

programming.

Course Learning Outcomes

Learning Outcomes

Identify advanced aspects of the hardware design verification process.

Apply IP-based verification and integration in subsystems.

Illustrate how UVM-based solutions are provided for Verification

environments.

Explain how UVM facilitates and enhances Verification component reuse.

Explain Verification’s role in an IP-based hierarchical Hardware Design

flow.

Apply current industry-standard tools to perform hierarchical Design

Functional Verification.

Explore and implement machine learning techniques to enhance

Illustrate how UVM-based solutions are provided for Verification

environments.

Explain how UVM facilitates and enhances Verification component reuse.

Explain Verification’s role in an IP-based hierarchical Hardware Design

flow.

Apply current industry-standard tools to perform hierarchical Design

Functional Verification.

Explore and implement machine learning techniques to enhance

verification efficiency and coverage.

Special Course Designation

None

This material Is: Required

ISBN:  978-1461407140
